question: Can task-level claiming reduce concurrent session duplication at N>=3?
design: Extend claim.py with task fingerprint claiming (600s TTL), integrate into task_order.py (deprioritize claimed tasks -100 score), add session heartbeat broadcasting to orient.py for cross-session visibility.
expect: Three gaps exist: (1) no task-level claiming, (2) identical task recommendations across sessions, (3) no concurrent session visibility. Building all three should enable automatic task divergence.
actual: All three mechanisms built and tested: claim-task/check-task/release-task/list-tasks (task claims), --claim-top (auto-claim in task_order.py), heartbeat/sessions (session broadcasting), orient.py concurrent activity display. Claimed tasks show [CLAIMED] with score -100. Sessions visible in orient output.
diff: No prediction errors — gaps were structural and obvious. Implementation was straightforward extension of existing claim.py patterns. Key design choice: 600s TTL for tasks (5x file claim TTL) because tasks last minutes not seconds.

task_order.py.behavior: Claimed tasks get score -100 and tier suffix [CLAIMED]
